text: The Apple Watch carbon-neutral ruling raised governance concerns about the substantiation and oversight of environmental marketing claims.
The filing stated that new product launch timing remains unchanged despite a slower customer approval cycle.
Amazon's report explains that sustainability coverage spans subsidiaries and business units, which is a reporting scope statement rather than a specific ESG risk event.
The board discussed dividend coverage ratios after weaker operating margin in the consumer segment.
The company rolled out new supplier labor standards covering wage disclosure, rest periods, and corrective action plans.
Management said dividend payments would remain steady while inventory adjustment actions continue through year-end.
Los Angeles County sued PepsiCo and Coca-Cola over allegations that the companies misled the public about plastic bottle recyclability and environmental impacts.
Environmental regulators opened a review after repeated pollution events at the company's coastal processing plant.
Management said its climate transition plan now prioritizes renewable energy procurement to lower Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions over the next three years.
Starbucks identifies supply chain sustainability and climate-related risks in its annual reporting, indicating governance attention to operational and sourcing risks.
Unilever states that human rights remain a core part of its sustainability agenda and are connected to how the company manages social impact across operations.
The issuer restated prior disclosures after identifying misleading disclosure around vendor due diligence controls.
Walmart says its sustainability priorities include supporting people in supply chains through responsible sourcing and inclusive economic opportunity.
The company reported higher greenhouse gas emissions from its manufacturing operations.
The operating review said local authorities cited the company for stormwater pollution linked to waste handling failures.
German prosecutors fined DWS after finding that the asset manager made misleading ESG-related statements in marketing and documentation.
Nike's sustainability data discusses injury rates and worker safety metrics covering full-time and certain temporary workers, indicating ongoing tracking of workplace safety performance.
The company believes its pricing strategy will protect market share during a more promotional quarter.
Apple describes its Apple 2030 plan as a strategy to reduce greenhouse gas emissions through recycled materials, renewable energy, lower-carbon transport, and carbon removal.
The company announced a new employee wellness policy.
The annual filing said the audit committee reviewed a material weakness in internal controls over contract approvals.
Regulators requested documents after the company disclosed weaknesses in anti-bribery monitoring.
